preamble: The palatalization of the alveolar lateral [l] to the palatal lateral [ʎ] is a widespread historical phonetic shift, particularly in the development of Romance languages (e.g., Latin 'filia' > Italian 'figlia' [ʎ]). Triggered by a following high front vowel or palatal glide [j], this process often results in the loss of the alveolar contact in favor of a dorsal contact against the hard palate.

examples.note: Spanish historically went through a [ʎ] stage before shifting to [x].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
